briefdescription.para: prevent circular inclusions by using protection macros
This value is the default polling interval defined in milliseconds.

detaileddescription.para: The polling interval defines the amount of time to wait between successive reads of the RxStatus register.

briefdescription.para: Needed storage to transmit/receive messages to the HDCP2.2 receiver This includes 1 extra byte for the DDC address used.
Needed storage for the Device IDs in the revocation list.
The list of maximum pairing info items to store.
The size of the log buffer.
Get the DP AUX register data.
Set the DP AUX register data.
These constants are used to identify callback functions.
No Rx Attached.
Transmit Low value content.
Known HDCP2 Capable Rx.
Exchange Km.
substate of A1: wait for AKE_SEND_CERT.
No stored Km substate of A1: wait for AKE_SEND_H_PRIME.
No stored Km substate of A1, wait for AKE_SEND_PAIRING_INFO.
Stored substate of A1, wait for AKE_SEND_PAIRING_INFO.
Locality Check.
Locality Check.
Exchange Ks.
Test for repeater.
Authenticated.
Wait for receiver ID list, verify and send acknowledgment.
Wait for receiver ID list.
Verify Receiver ID List.
Send Receiver ID List acknowledgment.
Content Stream Management.
Content Stream Management.
Number of states in the state machine.
These constants specify the different states in the internal state machine.
A HDCP2 compatible receiver is not found.
Authentication is busy.
Authentication is completed successfully.
Authentication failed.
ReAuthentication requested.
A device in the HDCP chain is revoked.
No valid SRM is loaded.
These constants specify return values on polling with XHdcp22Tx_Dp_Poll.

detaileddescription.para: Depending on this return value, encryption of the HDMI signal should be set to encrypted or un-encrypted. See also XHdcp22Tx_Dp_EnableEncryption and XHdcp22Tx_Dp_DisableEncryption

briefdescription.para: Type 0 Content Stream.

detaileddescription.para: Stream may be transmitted to all HDCP devices.

briefdescription.para: Type 1 Content Stream.

detaileddescription.para: Stream must not be transmitted to HDCP1.x devices and HDCP2.0 Repeaters.

briefdescription.para: These constants are used to define the content stream type.
Log Event None.
State of the state machine.
Authentication result of polling.
HDCP2.2 core is enabled or disabled.
HDCP2.2 core is reset.
HDCP2.2 stream is encrypted or not.
An error was detected in one of the test modes.
Log event for debugging.
Number of times Locality check has been done.
Number of times Content Stream Management check has been done.
User logging.
Last value the list, only used for checking.
These constants are events as stored in the logging list.
Display Port protocol.
HDMI protocol.
These constants are used to define the used protocol.
Module acts as a HDCP 2.2 transmitter.
Module acts as a HDCP 2.2 repeater.
Module acts as a HDCP 2.2 converter.
These constants are used to define the used mode.
These constants are used to identify fields inside the topology structure.
Callback type for status.

detaileddescription.para: Callback type used for pointer to single input function.

parameterdescription.para: is a callback reference passed in by the upper layer when setting the callback functions, and passed back to the upper layer when the callback is invoked.

memberdef.definition: typedef int(* XHdcp22_Tx_DdcHandler)(u8 DeviceAddress, u16 ByteCount, u8 *BufferPtr, u8 Stop, void *RefPtr)
memberdef.argsstring: )(u8 DeviceAddress, u16 ByteCount, u8 *BufferPtr, u8 Stop, void *RefPtr)
memberdef.name: XHdcp22_Tx_DdcHandler

briefdescription.para: Callback type used for calling DDC read and write functions.

parameterdescription.para: is the (i2c) device address of the HDCP port.
is the amount of data bytes in the buffer to read or write.
is a pointer to a buffer that is used for reading or writing.
is a flag to control if a stop token is set or not.
is a callback reference passed in by the upper layer when setting the DDC reading and writing functions, and passed back to the upper layer when the callback is invoked.

listitem.para: XST_SUCCESS The read action was successful.

memberdef.definition: typedef u32(* Xhdcp22_Tx_Dp_RdWrHandler)(void *HandlerRef, u32 offset, u8 *buf, u32 size)
memberdef.name: Xhdcp22_Tx_Dp_RdWrHandler

briefdescription.para: Type for pointer to four input function with a return value.
This function returns a reference to an XHdcp22_Tx_Dp_Config structure based on the core id, DeviceId.

detaileddescription.para: The return value will refer to an entry in the device configuration table defined in the xhdcp22_tx_g.c file.

parameterdescription.para: is the unique core ID of the HDCP2.2 TX core for the lookup operation.

simplesect.para: XHdcp22Tx_Dp_LookupConfig returns a reference to a config record in the configuration table (in xhdcp22_tx_g.c) corresponding to DeviceId, or NULL if no match is found.

briefdescription.para: This function initializes the HDCP22 TX core.

detaileddescription.para: This function must be called prior to using the HDCP TX core. Initialization of the HDCP TX includes setting up the instance data and ensuring the hardware is in a quiescent state.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XHdcp22_Tx_Dp core instance.
points to the configuration structure associated with the HDCP TX core.
is the base address of the device. If address translation is being used, then this parameter must reflect the virtual base address. Otherwise, the physical address should be used.

listitem.para: XST_FAILURE Initialization of the internal timer failed or there was a HDCP TX PIO ID mismatch.

briefdescription.para: This function resets the state machine.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XHdcp22Tx core instance.

briefdescription.para: This function clear the global pairing info structure, so every HDCP2.2 receiver will have to go through the 'no stored km' sequence to authenticate.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XHdcp22Tx core instance.

briefdescription.para: This function is a called to start authentication.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XHdcp22Tx core instance.

listitem.para: XST_SUCCESS if authenticated is started successfully.
XST_FAIL if the state machine is disabled.

briefdescription.para: This function is a executed every time to trigger the state machine.

detaileddescription.para: The user of HDCP22 TX is responsible to call this function on a regular base.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XHdcp22Tx core instance.

simplesect.para: Enumerated authentication status defined in XHdcp22_Tx_Dp_AuthenticationType.

briefdescription.para: This function enables the state machine and acts as a resume.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XHdcp22Tx core instance.

briefdescription.para: This function disables the state machine and acts as a pause.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XHdcp22Tx core instance.

briefdescription.para: This function enables HDMI stream encryption by enabling the cipher.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XHdcp22Tx core instance.

simplesect.para:
- XST_SUCCESS if authenticated is started successfully.
XST_FAIL if the state machine is disabled.

briefdescription.para: This function disables HDMI stream encryption by disabling the cipher.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XHdcp22Tx core instance.

briefdescription.para: This function enables the blank output for the cipher.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XHdcp22Tx core instance.

briefdescription.para: This function disables the blank output for the cipher.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XHdcp22Tx core instance.

briefdescription.para: This function returns the current enabled state.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XHdcp22Tx core instance.

briefdescription.para: This function returns the current encryption enabled state.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XHdcp22Tx core instance.

simplesect.para: TRUE if encryption is enabled otherwise FALSE.

briefdescription.para: This function returns the current progress state.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XHdcp22Tx core instance.

simplesect.para: TRUE if authentication is in progress otherwise FALSE.

briefdescription.para: This function returns the current authenticated state.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XHdcp22Tx core instance.

briefdescription.para: This function checks if the downstream device HDCP2Version register is set.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XHdcp22Tx core instance.

simplesect.para:
- TRUE if downstream device is HDCP 2.2 capable.
FALSE if downstream device is not HDCP 2.2 capable.

briefdescription.para: This function reads the version.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XHdcp22_Tx_Dp core instance.

simplesect.para: Returns the version register of the cipher.

briefdescription.para: This function installs callback functions for the given HandlerType:

para.preformatted: HandlerType Callback Function Type ------------------------- --------------------------- (XHDCP22_TX_HANDLER_DDC_WRITE) DdcWrite (XHDCP22_TX_HANDLER_DDC_READ) DdcRead (XHDCP22_TX_HANDLER_AUTHENTICATED) AuthenticatedCallback (XHDCP22_TX_HANDLER_UNAUTHENTICATED) UnauthenticatedCallback (XHDCP22_TX_HANDLER_DOWNSTREAM_TOPOLOGY_AVAILABLE) DownstreamTopologyAvailableCallback

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the HDMI RX core instance.
specifies the type of handler.
is the address of the callback function.
is a user data item that will be passed to the callback function when it is invoked.

listitem.para: XST_SUCCESS if callback function installed successfully.

simplesect.para: Invoking this function for a handler that already has been installed replaces it with the new handler.

briefdescription.para: This function returns the pointer to the internal timer control instance needed for connecting the timer interrupt to an interrupt controller.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XHdcp22Tx core instance.

simplesect.para: A pointer to the internal timer control instance.

briefdescription.para: This function clears the log pointers.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XHdcp22_Tx_Dp core instance.
allows to add debug logging.

briefdescription.para: This function writes HDCP TX logs into buffer.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XHdcp22_Tx_Dp core instance.
specifies an action to be carried out. Please refer XHdcp22_Tx_Dp_LogEvt enum in xhdcp22_tx_dp.h.
specifies the information that gets written into log buffer.

briefdescription.para: This function provides the log information from the log buffer.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XHdcp22_Tx_Dp core instance.

listitem.para: Content of log buffer if log pointers are not equal.

briefdescription.para: This function prints the content of log buffer.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the HDCP22 TX core instance.

briefdescription.para: This function returns the time expired since a log reset was called.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XHdcp22_Tx_Dp core instance.

simplesect.para: The expired logging time in useconds.

briefdescription.para: This function can be used to change the polling value.

detaileddescription.para: The polling value is the amount of time in milliseconds to wait between successive reads of the RxStatus register. The RxStatus register is polled to determine when a message is available for reading during authentication or during the link integrity check phase to determine when to issue re-authentication. The polling value needs to be at most 20ms to account for the locality check.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XHdcp22Tx core instance.

is the polling interval defined in milliseconds
0 : Always Poll
1 : Poll after 1ms
2 : Poll after 2ms
3 : Poll after 3ms etc...

briefdescription.para: This function prints the state machine information.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the HDCP22 TX core instance.

briefdescription.para: This function is used to load the Lc128 value by copying the contents of the array referenced by Lc128Ptr into the cipher.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XHdcp22_Tx_Dp core instance.
is a pointer to an array.

briefdescription.para: This function is used to load the system renewability messages (SRMs) which carries the Receiver ID revocation list.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XHdcp22_Tx_Dp core instance.
is a pointer to an array.

listitem.para: XST_FAILURE if SRM signature verification failed.

briefdescription.para: This function returns a pointer to the Revocation Receiver ID list.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XHdcp22_Tx_Dp instance.

briefdescription.para: This function searches for the specified ReceiverID in the revocation list.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XHdcp22_Tx_Dp core instance.
is a pointer to the ReceiverID to lookup

listitem.para: TRUE if Receiver ID was found in the revocation list
FALSE if Receiver ID was not found in the revocation list

briefdescription.para: This function adds a ReceiverID to RevocationList.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XHdcp22Tx core instance.
is a pointer to a 5-byte receiver Id.

simplesect.para: Supposed to be called by Upstream Content Control Function

briefdescription.para: This function returns a pointer to the downstream topology structure.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XHdcp22_Tx_Dp core instance.

simplesect.para: A pointer to the XHdcp22_Tx_Dp_Topology structure or NULL when the topology info is invalid.

briefdescription.para: This function returns a pointer to the ReceiverID list in the repeater topology structure.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XHdcp22_Tx_Dp core instance.

simplesect.para: A pointer to the ReceiverID list in the topology structure.

briefdescription.para: This function is used to get various fields inside the topology structure.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XHdcp22_Tx_Dp core instance.
indicates what field of the topology structure to get.

simplesect.para: Value associated to field inside the topology structure.

briefdescription.para: This function returns the current repeater mode status.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XHdcp22_Tx_Dp instance.

listitem.para: TRUE if the HDCP 2.2 instance is part of the downstream port of a repeater
FALSE if the HDCP 2.2 instance is a transmitter

briefdescription.para: This function sets the repeater mode status.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XHdcp22_Tx_Dp instance.
is TRUE to enable the repeater mode and FALSE to disable.

briefdescription.para: This function sets the Content Stream Type.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XHdcp22_Tx_Dp instance.
specifies the content stream type.

briefdescription.para: This function is to attach the HDCP22 timer instance to DP TX subsystem's timer.

detaileddescription.para: For the DP design the timer is being used from the DP TX subsystem. So the HDCP22 timer instance will be attached in DpTxSs initialization.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XHdcp22_Tx_Dp core instance.
is the DpTx susbsystem's timer instance.

simplesect.para: For the DP design the timer is being used from the DP TX subsystem. So the HDCP22 timer instance will be attached in DpTxSs initialization.

briefdescription.para: This function is to set the protocol (HDMI/DP) over which HDCP22 is happening.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XHdcp22_Tx_Dp core instance.
is the protocol HDMI/DP.

briefdescription.para: This function handles timer interrupts.

parameterdescription.para: refers to a XHdcp22_Tx_Dp structure
the number of used timer.

simplesect.para: For dp design, this will be called form the Tx subsystem's timer handler so it should be a public function.

briefdescription.para: This function is to read the RX_STATUS when a cp_irq received.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XHdcp22_Tx_Dp core instance.

briefdescription.para: This function set the lane count of an hdcp22 interface.

parameterdescription.para: is the transmitter instance.
is the number of lanes of the interface.

briefdescription.para: This function set the MST mode of an hdcp22 interface.

parameterdescription.para: is the transmitter instance.
enables the MST configuration of the interface.
